site.name: linux-meta
site.tagline: Open metadata layer for Linux packages. v0.1 · early access.

hero.sub: Built in public. v0.1 · early access. Today we index software from 4 repositories and organise an open catalog for download and reuse. Original project work is still small and documented below.
hero.version: v0.1 · {count} packages indexed (redistributed from upstream repositories)
hero.metricsNote: Imported = redistributed from upstream repositories. AI = generated by project workers. Human = reviewed by an identified contributor.
hero.demoCap: live · each card shows rating origin

why.title: Linux ships software to children without saying what it ships.
why.p1: Several countries are starting to require age labeling for digital content. Linux distributions, package managers and stores like Flathub do not have a unified metadata layer for this today. OARS exists in the AppStream standard but is filled in by a fraction of packages.
why.p2: We don't claim to solve this alone. We build a redistribution and canonicalization layer on top of what already exists (Manjaro, Flathub, AUR, Debian) and start adding original work where there are gaps.
why.stateNote: Each number comes from the project database and will be published with the transparency snapshots.
why.quote: Every data point on the site carries visible origin. Every metric separates redistribution from original work. Every promise we haven't kept yet is listed at /transparency.

what.title: A complete dataset with field-level provenance.
what.sub: Each package has a public page and a matching record in the dataset. Same structure, documented source and a format designed for reuse by distributions and tools.
what.f1: Suggested minimum age (0-18), with visible origin: imported OARS, local AI or human reviewer.
what.f2: Detailed OARS 1.1 categories — violence, language, gambling, in-app purchases.
what.f3: Which official repositories carry the package, with per-distro versions.
what.f4: Languages the description exists in (most are imported from the author's upstream metainfo).
what.f5: Upstream project state — active, maintained, or abandoned.
what.f6: How tight the Flatpak / Snap sandbox is, in low/medium/high risk.

how.title: Transparent pipeline. Every step open source.
how.s1b: Workers collect metadata from Manjaro, Flathub, AUR and Debian. Then we dedupe by canonical_slug: Firefox remains Firefox on any distro.
how.s2b: When upstream OARS exists, we preserve it. Where there is a gap, workers suggest a rating and human review can confirm or correct it.
how.s3b: The site shows the data with origin and the full dataset enables local integration. People decide better; distributions integrate with less manual work.

who.title: Four audiences. Same base data.
who.fBody: Understand age, description and origin before installing software at home.
who.sBody: Choose software with more context for labs, lessons and internal policy.
who.dBody: Reuse ratings, translations and provenance without creating everything from scratch.
who.gBody: Use an open base for research, public procurement, training and audits.

sup.title: Three ways. The first is the most useful right now.
sup.sub: We don't ask for money while no accountability channel is in place. Use it, give feedback, contribute technically — that's worth more right now.
sup.useBody: See whether the page helps you decide. Found something confusing, wrong or incomplete? That feedback matters.
sup.buildBody: One reviewed rating, translated description or corrected origin already improves the dataset for everyone.
sup.sponsorBody: Responsible sponsorship needs accountability. The financial channel only opens when transparency is ready.

faq.title: Plain answers. No makeup.
faq.a1: We first preserve OARS ratings from upstream. Where OARS is missing, the project can suggest a rating and keep the origin visible until human review.
faq.q2: How do I use this in my distro?
faq.a2: Use the full snapshot when it is available on the transparency page. The goal is local consumption without depending on a linux-meta API.
faq.a3: The goal is traceability. Each field should show origin, method and review state so anyone can contest it.
faq.a4: The financial channel still depends on public accountability. Until then, the most useful contribution is reviewing, translating, testing and reporting errors.

lists.title: Packages filtered by context
lists.sub: Collections with age filter applied automatically. Each item links to the public page with full rating origin.

kids.title: Kids 6 to 9
kids.sub: Apps with rating ≤9 imported from official OARS. Packages not yet rated do not appear here.

foss.title: FOSS alternatives
foss.sub: Open replacements for popular proprietary software. Rating shown per item.

school.title: For schools
school.sub: Packages with rating ≤14 or not yet rated, useful in school labs — productivity, science and art.

creative.title: Multimedia creation
creative.sub: Audio, video, design and 3D modeling at professional level.

search.title: Browse packages
search.placeholder: Search by name (e.g. firefox, gimp, krita)
search.semanticUnavailable: Semantic search unavailable, falling back to text.

pkg.provRatingOars: Official OARS · imported from upstream AppStream
pkg.provTrUpstream: Imported from the author's upstream metainfo
